content.problemStatement:
A **weighted directed graph** consists of a set VVV of **vertices** and a collection EEE of **edges**. Each edge e‚ąąEe \in Ee‚ąąE is an ordered triple e=(x,y,w)e = (x, y, w)e=(x,y,w) where x‚ąąVx \in Vx‚ąąV and y‚ąąVy \in Vy‚ąąV are vertices, called the **start** and **end** vertices of eee, respectively, and www is a number called the **weight** of eee.

A **walk** from vertex sss to vertex ttt is an alternating sequence of vertices and edges (v1,e1,v2,e2,‚Ä¶,ek‚ąí1,vk)(v_1, e_1, v_2, e_2, \ldots, e_{k-1}, v_k)(v1‚Äč,e1‚Äč,v2‚Äč,e2‚Äč,‚Ä¶,ek‚ąí1‚Äč,vk‚Äč) (there are kkk vertices and k‚ąí1k - 1k‚ąí1 edges in this sequence) satisfying the following:


k‚Č•1k \ge 1k‚Č•1;
v1=sv_1 = sv1‚Äč=s;
vk=tv_k = tvk‚Äč=t;
The start vertex of eie_iei‚Äč is viv_ivi‚Äč (for each iii from 111 to k‚ąí1k - 1k‚ąí1);
The end vertex of eie_iei‚Äč is vi+1v_{i+1}vi+1‚Äč (for each iii from 111 to k‚ąí1k - 1k‚ąí1).
You are given a special weighted directed graph (V,E)(V, E)(V,E). The weights of the edges in this graph are **complex numbers**, that is, numbers of the form a+bia + bia+bi. Each complex weight a+bia + bia+bi satisfies the following:


aaa and bbb are integers;
a2+b2<5a^2 + b^2 < 5a2+b2<5.
We say that a walk from sss to ttt is **real** if the **product** of the weights of the edges in it is a real number. We call this product the **cost** of the walk.

Given two vertices sss and ttt, is there a **real** walk from sss to ttt? If so, what is the minimum possible cost of any real walk from sss to ttt? Note that:


It may be possible for the costs of the real walks to be arbitrarily small; you must also detect if this is the case.
If aaa and bbb are integers for every edge weight a+bia + bia+bi, then it can be shown that the cost of any real walk is an integer. In particular, the minimum possible cost is an integer.
The minimum possible cost may be very large, so only output it modulo 998244353998244353998244353.
The product of two complex numbers a+bia + bia+bi and a‚Ä≤+b‚Ä≤ia' + b'ia‚Ä≤+b‚Ä≤i is defined by multiplying them via the distributive property and then reducing i2=‚ąí1i^2 = -1i2=‚ąí1. For example:

(3+4i)‚čÖ(4‚ąí7i)=3‚čÖ4‚ąí3‚čÖ7i+4i‚čÖ4‚ąí4i‚čÖ7i=12‚ąí21i+16i‚ąí28i2=12‚ąí21i+16i‚ąí28(‚ąí1)=40‚ąí5i.\begin{align*}
(3 + 4i)\cdot (4 - 7i)
&= 3\cdot 4 - 3\cdot 7i + 4i\cdot 4 - 4i\cdot 7i \\
&= 12 - 21i + 16i - 28i^2 \\
&= 12 - 21i + 16i - 28(-1) \\
&= 40 - 5i. 
\end{align*}(3+4i)‚čÖ(4‚ąí7i)‚Äč=3‚čÖ4‚ąí3‚čÖ7i+4i‚čÖ4‚ąí4i‚čÖ7i=12‚ąí21i+16i‚ąí28i2=12‚ąí21i+16i‚ąí28(‚ąí1)=40‚ąí5i.‚ÄčWe say that a complex number a+bia + bia+bi is real if b=0b = 0b=0. In this case, we consider a+bia + bia+bi equal to the real number aaa.

For real numbers m>0m > 0m>0 and aaa, we define (a‚Äämod‚Ääm)(a \bmod m)(amodm) as the unique real number a‚Ä≤a'a‚Ä≤ such that 0‚Č§a‚Ä≤<m0 \le a' < m0‚Č§a‚Ä≤<m and a‚ąía‚Ä≤a - a'a‚ąía‚Ä≤ is divisible by mmm.


Input Format
The first line contains TTT, the number of test cases. The following lines describe the test cases.


The first line of each test case contains two space-separated integers ‚ą£V‚ą£|V|‚ą£V‚ą£ and ‚ą£E‚ą£|E|‚ą£E‚ą£, where ‚ą£V‚ą£|V|‚ą£V‚ą£ denotes the number of vertices in VVV and ‚ą£E‚ą£|E|‚ą£E‚ą£ denotes the number of edges in EEE. For simplicity, the vertices are numbered 111 to ‚ą£V‚ą£|V|‚ą£V‚ą£.


The second line contains two space-separated integers sss and ttt representing two vertices (which may be equal).


The next ‚ą£E‚ą£|E|‚ą£E‚ą£ lines describe the edges. Each of the next ‚ą£E‚ą£|E|‚ą£E‚ą£ lines contains four space-separated integers xxx, yyy, aaa and bbb denoting that (x,y,a+bi)(x, y, a + bi)(x,y,a+bi) is an edge.



Output Format
For each test case, output a single line containing:



An integer denoting the minimum possible cost of any real walk from sss to ttt. (This number must be reduced modulo 998244353998244353998244353.)
The string `NEGATIVE INFINITY` if the costs of the real walks are arbitrarily small.
The string `NEGATIVE` if there is no real walk from sss to ttt.
constraints: The sum of the ‚ą£V‚ą£|V|‚ą£V‚ą£ in a single file is ‚Č§3‚čÖ105\le 3\cdot 10^5‚Č§3‚čÖ105
The sum of the ‚ą£E‚ą£|E|‚ą£E‚ą£ in a single file is ‚Č§3‚čÖ105\le 3\cdot 10^5‚Č§3‚čÖ105
There may be edges with equal start and end vertices.
There may be multiple edges between the same pair of vertices.
